🧩 PROBLEM 1 — Fix the Code
Topic Combination

Variables + Streams + Strings

🎯 Objective
Fix the program so it correctly reads a full name and prints it with its length.
🧠 Logic
The program should:
Ask the user for their full name
Read the full line
Print the name
Print how many characters the name has
❌ Broken Code

#include <iostream>
#include <string>
using namespace std;

int main()
{
string name;
cout << "Enter your full name: ";
cin >> name;
cout << "Hello " << name << endl;
int len = length(name);
cout << "Your name has " << len << " characters." << endl;
return 0;
}

💡 Hint
cin >> only reads one word
length() belongs to the string object

------
🧩 PROBLEM 2 — Fix the Code
Topic Combination
Functions + Prototypes + Streams
🎯 Objective
Fix the program so the function correctly returns the square of a number.
🧠 Logic
User enters a number
Function computes the square
Program prints the result
❌ Broken Code

#include <iostream>
using namespace std;

square(int x);

int main()
{
int n;
cout << "Enter a number: ";
cin >> n;

cout << "Square = " << square << endl;

return 0;
}

int square(int x)
{
x * x;
}

💡 Hint
Three mistakes exist:
missing return type
function not called correctly
missing return statement

------

🧩 PROBLEM 3 — Fix the Code
Topic Combination
Formatting + Manipulators + Streams
🎯 Objective
Fix the program so it prints numbers in aligned columns.
🧠 Logic
Output should look like:
Number    Square
----------------
5        25
8        64
❌ Broken Code
#include <iostream>
using namespace std;

int main()
{
int a = 5;
int b = 8;
cout << setw(10) << "Number" << "Square" << endl;
cout << "----------------" << endl;
cout << setw(10) << a << a * a << endl;
cout << setw(10) << b << b * b << endl;
return 0;
}

💡 Hint
Missing a header file
Use setw() for both columns

-------

💻 PROBLEM 4 — Write a Program
Topic Combination
Functions + Constants + Input/Output

🎯 Objective
Create a program that calculates the area of a circle.
🧠 Logic
Ask user for radius
Use a constant for PI
Use a function to compute area
Print the result with 2 decimal places
Formula:
Area = π × r²

📋 Requirements
Use const double PI
Use a function:
double circleArea(double radius);
Format output using:
fixed
setprecision(2)

💡 Hint
You already used something similar in Chapter 2 exercises.
--------

💻 PROBLEM 5 — Write a Program
Topic Combination
Strings + Formatting + Streams
🎯 Objective
Create a small user profile formatter.
🧠 Logic
Program asks:

Enter your name:
Enter your age:
Enter your height:

Then prints:
User Profile
-------------------------
Name:      Patrick
Age:       20
Height:    5.80

📋 Requirements
Use:
getline() for name
setw() for alignment
setprecision(2) for height

💡 Hint
Use:
left
setw()

---------

💻 PROBLEM 6 — Write a Program
Topic Combination

Random Numbers + Functions + Loops

🎯 Objective

Create a random number generator program that runs until the user exits.

🧠 Logic

Program should:
Ask for a seed value
Generate 3 random numbers

Ask user:
Generate again? (y/n)
If y → repeat
If n → exit

Example output:

Random numbers: 34 81 12
Generate again? y
Random numbers: 45 90 17
Generate again? n
Program ended.
📋 Requirements
Use:
srand()
rand()
while loop
a function
Example function idea:
void generateRandom();
💡 Hint

Use:
rand() % 100
to keep numbers 0–99.
